name: Syntheteria: Core Game Loop — Sequential Implementation
description: Implement the strictly sequential core game loop for Syntheteria, a first-person 4X factory game on a machine planet. The player grinds ore deposits into powder, compresses powder into material cubes, carries and stacks cubes to build walls and machines. Each system feeds directly into the next — the compression mechanic needs harvested powder, the grabber needs cubes to exist, stacking needs grabbable cubes, walls need stacked cubes.

userStories.title: Object Selection via Raycast
userStories.description: As a player in first-person view I want to look at any object and click/tap to select it So that I can see what it is and interact with it
acceptanceCriteria: Rapier raycast from camera center on left-click (desktop) or single-tap (mobile)
Hit entity gets `IsSelected` tag trait added
Selection highlight shader renders on selected entity
Previous selection cleared when new object selected
Ray max distance configurable via `config/rendering.json`
HUD shows selected object info (type, health, contents)

userStories.title: Contextual Action Menu
userStories.description: As a player with an object selected I want to see a radial menu of available actions for that object So that I can interact with it contextually
acceptanceCriteria: Radial menu appears near crosshair when object selected
Actions determined by selected entity's traits (e.g., OreDeposit shows "Harvest", MaterialCube shows "Grab"/"Stack")
Menu items are clickable/tappable with clear icons
Menu dismisses on: action selected, click away, ESC key, or new selection
Action availability checks (e.g., "Harvest" only if player has harvesting component)
Touch-friendly: minimum 44px tap targets

userStories.title: Ore Deposit Harvesting
userStories.description: As a player near an ore deposit I want to grind it into powder that accumulates in my hopper So that I can later compress it into building cubes
acceptanceCriteria: OreDeposit entities placed in world with material type (iron, copper, stone, carbon)
Player must be within `config/mining.json → harvestRange` meters
Harvesting is continuous while held (not instant) — takes `grindTimePerUnit` seconds per unit
Powder accumulates in player's PowderStorage trait
OreDeposit has finite quantity that depletes
Visual feedback: particle sparks while grinding, deposit shrinks
Audio: grinding sound loops during harvest
Deposit material type determines powder type → determines cube properties

userStories.title: Powder Compression into Cubes
userStories.description: As a player with powder in my hopper I want to compress it into a solid 0.5m material cube So that I can use it for building
acceptanceCriteria: Compression action available when player has enough powder (amount per `config/cubeMaterials.json`)
Compression takes time: `compressionTime` seconds per cube
Creates a new MaterialCube entity with Rapier rigid body (0.5m box collider)
Cube spawns at player's feet, affected by gravity
Cube inherits material type from powder (iron cube, stone cube, etc.)
Different materials have different properties (weight, durability, conductivity)
Visual: cube has PBR material matching its type
Compression progress bar shown in HUD

userStories.title: Grab, Carry, Drop, Throw Cubes
userStories.description: As a player looking at a cube I want to pick it up, carry it, and place or throw it So that I can move cubes to where I need them
acceptanceCriteria: "Grab" action in radial menu when looking at a MaterialCube within reach
Grabbed cube attaches to player (follows camera, offset in front)
Cube's Rapier body switches to kinematic while held
"Drop" places cube gently at feet
"Throw" launches cube in look direction with configurable force
Only one cube held at a time
Carrying a cube slows player movement by `config/botMovement.json → carrySpeedMultiplier`
Visual: held cube rendered in front of camera

userStories.title: Furnace Machine
userStories.description: As a player who has built a furnace I want to smelt cubes into refined materials So that I can create stronger building materials
acceptanceCriteria: Furnace is a placeable machine (from radial menu on empty ground)
Furnace has input hopper (accepts cubes via "Insert" action)
Smelting takes time per `config/furnace.json → smeltTimePerCube`
Furnace requires power connection (wire from lightning rod)
Output: refined cube with improved properties (higher durability, different appearance)
Furnace recipes defined in `config/furnace.json`
Visual: furnace glows when active, smoke particles

userStories.title: Cube Snap-Stacking on 0.5m Grid
userStories.description: As a player carrying a cube I want to stack it precisely on other cubes with grid snapping So that I can build walls and structures
acceptanceCriteria: When holding a cube near another cube/ground, show ghost preview snapped to 0.5m grid
Ghost shows valid (green) or invalid (red) placement
Place action locks cube into grid position
Placed cubes get `PlacedAt` trait with grid coordinates
Stacked cubes form a CubeStack (group entity)
Physics: placed cubes become static (no longer affected by gravity)
Structural integrity: unsupported cubes fall (need foundation or adjacent support)
Max stack height configurable in config

userStories.title: Wall Building from Stacked Cubes
userStories.description: As a player who has stacked cubes I want to designate a wall line and auto-fill it with cubes from my inventory So that I can efficiently build defensive walls
acceptanceCriteria: "Build Wall" action when looking at placed cube
Player marks start and end points for wall line
System calculates required cubes and shows ghost preview
Player confirms → cubes auto-place along the line (consumes from carried/nearby stockpile)
Wall inherits material properties (stone walls = high durability, metal = conductivity)
Walls block enemy pathfinding and projectiles
Walls can be damaged and breached (cube HP system)
Visual: wall cubes merge rendering for seamless appearance

userStories.title: Machine Assembly from Cube Patterns
userStories.description: As a player I want to arrange cubes in specific patterns to create machines So that I can build functional equipment like miners and fabricators
acceptanceCriteria: Specific cube arrangements recognized as machine blueprints
Blueprints defined in `config/buildings.json` (3D pattern + required materials)
When pattern matched, "Assemble" action appears in radial menu
Assembly converts cube stack into a functional machine entity
Machine inherits material properties from constituent cubes
Visual: assembly animation (cubes merge/transform into machine mesh)
Disassembly: machines can be broken back into cubes
